basicDetails.schemeName: Large cardamom Irrigation Land Development : Irrigation equipment (nos)

schemeContent.briefDescription: The scheme aims to motivate the growers to bring more area under irrigation by constructing water storage structures, rain water harvesting structures and installation of irrigation equipments for increasing the productivity and improving quality of large cardamom.
schemeContent.detailedDescription_md: The scheme “Large Cardamom Irrigation Land Development: Irrigation Equipment (Nos)” is a component of the Integrated Scheme for Export Promotion and Quality Improvement in Spices and Research and Development of Cardamom implemented during the 15th Finance Commission period (2021–22 to 2025–26) under the Export-Oriented Production component. The scheme aims to motivate growers to bring more area under irrigation by supporting the construction of water storage structures, rainwater harvesting systems, and the installation of irrigation equipment, thereby increasing productivity and improving the quality of large cardamom.
schemeContent.benefits_md: 1. 50 % of cost of equipment subject to a maximum of ₹ 15,000 for General and 75% subject to maximum of ₹ 20,000/- for SC/ST 1. Large cardamom growers having land holding size of 0.10 Ha to 8 Ha

children.text: The scheme “Large Cardamom Irrigation Land Development: Irrigation Equipment (Nos)” is a component of the Integrated Scheme for Export Promotion and Quality Improvement in Spices and Research and Development of Cardamom implemented during the 15th Finance Commission period (2021–22 to 2025–26) under the Export-Oriented Production component. The scheme aims to motivate growers to bring more area under irrigation by supporting the construction of water storage structures, rainwater harvesting systems, and the installation of irrigation equipment, thereby increasing productivity and improving the quality of large cardamom.
50 % of cost of equipment subject to a maximum of ₹ 15,000 for General and 75% subject to maximum of ₹  20,000/- for SC/ST
Large cardamom growers having land holding size of 0.10 Ha to 8 Ha
Registration
Step 1: The applicant should visit the
ServicesPlus
website.
Step 2: Click on the “
Register”
option on the homepage.
Step 3: Fill out the registration form by entering Full Name, Email ID, Mobile Number, Password, selecting State, and Captcha.
Step 4: Review all details carefully and submit the registration form.
Step 5: An OTP will be sent to your registered mobile number or email; enter the OTP and click “Verify.”
Step 6: Registration will be completed successfully, and a confirmation message will be displayed or sent via email.
Login
Step 1: The applicant should visit the ServicesPlus website and click on
“Login.”
Step 2: Enter your Email ID, Password, and Captcha.
Step 3: Select the relevant service/scheme name.
Step 4: Fill in the required details in the application form.
Step 5: Submit the application.

applicationProcess.process_md: **Registration** Step 1: The applicant should visit the [ServicesPlus](https://serviceonline.gov.in/dbt/) website. Step 2: Click on the “[Register” ](https://serviceonline.gov.in/dbt/citizenRegistration.html)option on the homepage. Step 3: Fill out the registration form by entering Full Name, Email ID, Mobile Number, Password, selecting State, and Captcha. Step 4: Review all details carefully and submit the registration form. Step 5: An OTP will be sent to your registered mobile number or email; enter the OTP and click “Verify.” Step 6: Registration will be completed successfully, and a confirmation message will be displayed or sent via email. **Login** Step 1: The applicant should visit the ServicesPlus website and click on [“Login.”](https://serviceonline.gov.in/dbt/loginWindow.do) Step 2: Enter your Email ID, Password, and Captcha. Step 3: Select the relevant service/scheme name. Step 4: Fill in the required details in the application form. Step 5: Submit the application.

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md: 1. The applicant should be a large cardamom grower located in Sikkim, Darjeeling and Kalimpong districts of West Bengal, or other North Eastern States. 1. The applicant should submit the application for consideration under the scheme in the prescribed format.

children.text: The applicant should be a large cardamom grower located in Sikkim, Darjeeling and Kalimpong districts of West Bengal, or other North Eastern States.
The applicant should submit the application for consideration under the scheme in the prescribed format.
